title: George van Heerden and Annerie Dercksen scoop top awards
description: OUDTSHOORN NEWS - Six Gun Grill Garden Route...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"Gallery","html":"Gallery"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - Six Gun Grill Garden Route Badgers, batting hero George van Heerden, won the prestigious Garden Route Badgers Player of the Season award at their annual awards breakfast ceremony that was held at the Wilderness Hotel in George on 21 June.","html":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - Six Gun Grill Garden Route Badgers, batting hero George van Heerden, won the prestigious Garden Route Badgers Player of the Season award at their annual awards breakfast ceremony that was held at the Wilderness Hotel in George on 21 June."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Proteas Women all-rounder, Annerie Dercksen, who excelled for the Proteas women’s team at international level and was a star player for the Garden Route Badgers women’s team in the Hollywoodbets professional women’s competition won the Garden Route Badgers Women’s player of the season award."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Van Heerden was the top runs scorer for the Badgers across all formats and was the overall top runs scorer in the Division 2 Four day competition. He scored 515 runs with a highest score of 174 at an average of 64.37 with two centuries and two half-centuries. Dercksen also received the Simon Swigelaar Floating Trophy for her outstanding achievements, including the award as ICC Newcomer of the Year for 2024.","html":"Van Heerden was the top runs scorer for the Badgers across all formats and was the overall top runs scorer in the Division 2 Four day competition. He scored 515 runs with a highest score of 174 at an average of 64.37 with two centuries and two half-centuries. Dercksen also received the Simon Swigelaar Floating Trophy for her outstanding achievements, including the award as ICC Newcomer of the Year for 2024."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Special Merit Awards","html":"Special Merit Awards"},{"type":"list","items":["Jezé Campher (Môrester Senior Secondary School) who received the award as batter- and all-rounder of the tournament in the Gold Section of the CSA Girls U16 National tournament that was hosted in Johannesburg during December 2024.","Mariana Breedt (Outeniqua High School) and Emmy-Kay Samson (Oudtshoorn School of Skills) for being selected for the SA Schools Fillies Girls U19 team that was selected upon conclusion of the CSA Girls U19 National tournament that was hosted in Durban during December 2024.","Enathi Kitshini (Thembalethu Hub), Shirwin Plaatjies (Bridgton Hub) and Keano Coerecius (Bridgton Hub) for being selected for the SA Hub team that participated in the Schools SA20 Knock-out competition.","Enathi Kitshini: (Thembalethu High) selected for the Southern Superstars team that participated in the CSA Cubs that was hosted in Stellenbosch during January 2025, the SA Schools XI that was selected upon conclusion of the CSA Khaya Majola u/19 National tournament that was hosted in Cape Town during December 2024 and the SA U19 team","Elridge Booysen was appointed as Assistant Coach for the SA Hub that participated in the"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Schools SA20 Knock-out Competition","html":"Schools SA20 Knock-out Competition"},{"type":"list","items":["Wynand de Ridder was appointed as head coach for the Southern Superstars team that participated in the CSA Cubs that was hosted in Stellenbosch in January 2025.","Pieter Stuurman was appointed as the head coach of the CSA Rural team","Anoxolo Kitshini selected for the CSA Rural team","Kerrin Klaaste, officiated on the CSA Reserve panel, officiated on the ICC International panel, appointed to officiate in the ICC Women’s U19 World Cup in Malaysia in January 2025. Appointed to officiate the international series in all formats between South Africa and England in December 2024. ICC men’s T20 International debut; September 2024 in Tanzania in September 2024.","André Olivier - officiated on the CSA First Class panel","Yolandi van der Westhuizen who was appointed as the head coach of the SA Schools Fillies Girls’U19 team that was selected upon conclusion of the CSA Girls U19 National tournament that was hosted in Durban during December 2024.","George van Heerden was selected for the SA Emerging team that toured Bangladesh in May and June 2025.","Mieke de Ridder - represented the Proteas in various white ball series - including the 2024 ICC T20 World Cup in the UAE.","Annerie Dercksen - represented the Proteas across all formats - including the 2024 ICC T20 World Cup in the UAE and she received the Awars as ICC Newcomer of the Year for 2024."],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"George van Heerden, won the prestigious Garden Route Badgers Player of the Season award","html":"George van Heerden, won the prestigious Garden Route Badgers Player of the Season award"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Three employees of the company, Albertus Kennedy (chief executive officer), Pieter Stuurman (club and facilities co-ordinator) and Zenobia Nel (executive assistant and human resource administrator) received Twenty Year Continuous Service Certificates.","html":"Three employees of the company, Albertus Kennedy (chief executive officer), Pieter Stuurman (club and facilities co-ordinator) and Zenobia Nel (executive assistant and human resource administrator) received Twenty Year Continuous Service Certificates."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The 1979 Southern Cape Schools team received a Lifetime Achievement award as well as six co-ordinators with more than twenty years of service in the Mini Cricket program; Shireen Noble (30 years), Regina Wicomb (30 years), Lavona Cloete (20 years), Glenda Stalmeester (20 years), Felicity Prins (20 years) and Meryl Daniels (20 years).","html":"The 1979 Southern Cape Schools team received a Lifetime Achievement award as well as six co-ordinators with more than twenty years of service in the Mini Cricket program; Shireen Noble (30 years), Regina Wicomb (30 years), Lavona Cloete (20 years), Glenda Stalmeester (20 years), Felicity Prins (20 years) and Meryl Daniels (20 years)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Several members who were involved in various capacities within the SWD Cricket structures who passed away during the past year were honored with a Roll of Honor Award.","html":"Several members who were involved in various capacities within the SWD Cricket structures who passed away during the past year were honored with a Roll of Honor Award."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Other achievements that were recognised are:","html":"Other achievements that were recognised are:"},{"type":"list","items":["Hadley Benjamin (LSEN Cricketer of the Season)","Alex Fouche (Primary Schools Cricketer of the Season)","Enathi Kitshini (High Schools Player of the Season)","Marianna Breedt and Emmy-Kay (High School Girl’s Player of the Season)","Debbie van Lill (SWD Scorer of the Season)","Lyndon Volkwyn (SWD Umpire of the Season)","Simon Hlwayi (from NMU Madibaz CC – the Club Groundsman of the Season)"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Union Stars CC was the winner of the Fair Play Award while Pacaltsdorp United CC won the Operational Excellence Award.","html":"Union Stars CC was the winner of the Fair Play Award while Pacaltsdorp United CC won the Operational Excellence Award."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Other achievements that were recognised are:","html":"Other achievements that were recognised are:"},{"type":"list","items":["Most Runs in the SA20 Schools U19 Girl’s: Kelsey Stuurman (Bridgton Hub)","Most Runs in the SA20 Schools U19 Boys: Kurt Botha (Rosemoor Hub)","Most Runs in U15 Promotion League: Qhamani Viewer (Thembalethu Hub)","Most Runs in U19 Promotion League: Austin Behr (Wittedrift High)","Most Runs in U19 Super League: Ducaine Plaatjies (Bridgton Hub)","Most Wickets in the SA20 Schools U19 Girl’s: Jezé Campher (Bridgton Hub)","Most Wickets in the SA20 Schools U19 Boys: Keano Witbooi (D’Almeida Hub)","Most Wickets in U15 Super League: Ross Taljaard (Outeniqua High)","Most Wickets in U19 Promotion League: Denzay Plaatjies (Oudtshoorn High)","Most Wickets in U19 Super League: Theodore Qusheka (Langenhoven Gymnasium)","Batter of the Season: Women Division 2 League: Camerodien Swartz (Knysna Cavaliers)","Bowler of the Season: Women Division 2 League: Camerodien Swartz (Knysna Cavaliers)","Batter of the Season: Women Twenty20 League: Kim Malgas (Knysna Cavaliers CC)","Bowler of the Season: Women Twenty20 League: Jané Winster (Union Stars CC)","Batsman of the Season: Twenty20 League: Joviano Tock (Union Stars CC)","Bowler of the Season: Twenty20 League: Ricardo Majolla (Pacaltsdorp United CC)","Batter of the Season: Women League: Haroline Rhodes (Union Stars United CC)","Bowler of the Season: Women League: Candice Reynolds (George CC)","Batsman of the Season: Reserve League Division 2: Carlton Rasmus (Beaufort West CC)","Bowler of the Season: Reserve League Division 2: Carlton Rasmus (Beaufort West CC)","Batsman of the Season: Reserve League Division 1: Keenan Arries (Union Stars CC)","Bowler of the Season: Reserve League Division 1: Vian Young (Hartenbos CC)","Batsman of the Season: Promotion League: Thabo Dwili (Thembalethu CC)","Bowler of the Season: Promotion League: Divino Koert (Ramblers CC)","Batsmen of the Season: Premier League: Warren Bell (Pacaltsdorp United CC)","Bowler of the Season: Premier League: Lee-Henry Meyer (Union Stars CC)"],"ordered":false},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The respective league and club winners also received their trophies."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Schools League","html":"Schools League"},{"type":"list","items":["Schools SA20 Girls U19: Bridgton Hub","Schools SA20 Boys U19: Bridgton Hub","U15 Promotion League: Oakdale U15B","U15 Super League: Outeniqua","U19 Promotion League: Wittedrift High School","U19 Super League: Bridgton Hub"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"League Winners","html":"League Winners"},{"type":"list","items":["Women Division 2 T20: Dysselsdorp","Women Division 1 T20: Union Stars","Women Division 2: Knysna Cavaliers","Women Division 1: George Women","Reserve Division 2: Beaufort West","Reserve Division 1: George B","Promotion League: New Dawn Park","T20: Pacaltsdorp United","Premier League: Union Stars I"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"PHOTO GALLERY:  George Van Heerden and Annerie Dercksen scoop Garden
